Description: Uncovered a GCP API key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Google Cloud services and data breaches.
Author: Michael Lewis
Message:
[CUR-1389][CUR-1391] Mobile runtime env resolution + build/CI delta record (#639)

## Summary
- `clinical_diary` resolves its environment (local/dev/qa/uat/prod) at
**runtime** from a bundled pointer asset (`assets/config/env.json`,
default `dev`) via a new `EnvProfile` model/registry, instead of a
compile-time `--dart-define=APP_FLAVOR`. One Dart compilation serves
every flavor; the four Android product flavors remain only to vary
`applicationId`, the bundled env asset, native service config, launcher
metadata, and signing. `AppConfig`, the bootstrap banner/title, and the
prod hard-off gate all derive from the resolved profile;
`lib/flavors.dart` is removed.
- Every mobile build/run path (Android signed + unsigned, iOS, IPA, dev
launchers) and the Fastlane `build_flavor` lane now stamp `env.json` for
the target env (via `tool/_write_env_pointer.sh`, restored on exit) and
no longer pass the inert `APP_FLAVOR` define — closing a wrong-backend
risk where a non-dev package could point at the dev backend. Web builds
keep `--dart-define=APP_FLAVOR` (separate concern).
- A controlled delta record (`_reports/reference/mobile-env-delta.md`)
is generated by `tool/generate_env_delta_record.sh`, enumerating the
entire per-env package delta and failing on any undeclared per-flavor
difference; mobile CI (`clinical_diary-ci.yml`) regenerates it as a
drift gate. Establishes a canonical `_reports/` tree (`reference/`
tracked, `build/` ignored).
- Authors the two anchoring REQs: `DIARY-OPS-single-promotable-artifact`
(single env-independent compilation + controlled delta, assertions C/D)
and `DIARY-DEV-runtime-environment-resolution`.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Description: Uncovered a JSON Web Token, which may lead to unauthorized access to web applications and sensitive user data.
Author: Michael Lewis
Message:
[CUR-1227] Add break-glass GCP access design spec and requirements (#542)

Phase design spec at `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-02-break-glass-design.md` captures the v1 architecture: reusable GitHub Actions workflows in `hht_diary` core (grant, sweep, doctor) plus a Slack notification composite, invoked from sponsor-side wrappers via `workflow_dispatch` with environment-gated approval for elevated grants.

**REQ-o00084** (`spec/ops-break-glass.md`) — operational obligations: `@anspar.org`-only bindings, 24h ceiling, denylist for privilege-escalation roles, approval gating with self-review prevention, contemporaneous Slack notification, audit-grade record retention.

**REQ-d00161** (`spec/dev-break-glass.md`) — implementation contract: transactional `set-iam-policy` with etag, doctor preflight gating, fixed Slack routing table in the composite, roster-driven identity resolution with Cloud Identity verification and either a CODEOWNERS rule or a branch-protection two-reviewer rule on the roster file, SHA-pinned cross-repo workflow references.

Also in this PR:
- `.github/workflows/ci-gate.yml` and `.github/workflows/pr-validation.yml` switched to marker-based, paginated, update-in-place PR comments. Existing duplicate "CI Gate" / "Requirements Validation Passed" comments self-heal on the next run instead of accumulating.
- `.gitignore` entry for `.claude/scheduled_tasks.lock` so `git worktree remove` succeeds without `--force`.

Companion / follow-up tickets:
- **CUR-1228** — Phase 1b: revoke standing GCP permissions, SA-based org admin (prerequisite for break-glass to be meaningful).
- **CUR-1253** — apply the PR-comment workflow fixes to `hht_diary_callisto`.
- **CUR-1256** — long-term audit-log architecture (per-env Cloud Logging, sponsor-aware routing, cost-aware retention escalator).
- **CUR-1258** — break-glass design refinements punch list from this PR's review (sweeper cadence, OIDC binding patterns, expires_at handling, etc).
- **CUR-647** — broader zero-trust GCP administration capability.

**REQ-o00084** (`spec/ops-break-glass.md`) — operational obligations: `@anspar.org`-only bindings, 24h ceiling, denylist for privilege-escalation roles, approval gating with self-review prevention, contemporaneous Slack notification, audit-grade record retention.

**REQ-d00161** (`spec/dev-break-glass.md`) — implementation contract: transactional `set-iam-policy` with etag, doctor preflight gating, fixed Slack routing table in the composite, roster-driven identity resolution with Cloud Identity verification and either a CODEOWNERS rule or a branch-protection two-reviewer rule on the roster file, SHA-pinned cross-repo workflow references.

Also in this PR:
- `.github/workflows/ci-gate.yml` and `.github/workflows/pr-validation.yml` switched to marker-based, paginated, update-in-place PR comments. Existing duplicate "CI Gate" / "Requirements Validation Passed" comments self-heal on the next run instead of accumulating.
- `.gitignore` entry for `.claude/scheduled_tasks.lock` so `git worktree remove` succeeds without `--force`.

Companion / follow-up tickets:
- **CUR-1228** — Phase 1b: revoke standing GCP permissions, SA-based org admin (prerequisite for break-glass to be meaningful).
- **CUR-1253** — apply the PR-comment workflow fixes to `hht_diary_callisto`.
- **CUR-1256** — long-term audit-log architecture (per-env Cloud Logging, sponsor-aware routing, cost-aware retention escalator).
- **CUR-1258** — break-glass design refinements punch list from this PR's review (sweeper cadence, OIDC binding patterns, expires_at handling, etc).
- **CUR-647** — broader zero-trust GCP administration capability.

Author: Michael Bushe
Message:
[CUR-734] Add OTel observability: otel_common package, Logs signal, custom metrics, portal instrumentation (#490)

  Summary

  - Create shared otel_common package with HTTP tracing middleware, DB query tracing, structured logging, and error reporting
  - Instrument diary_server with full OTel (traces, logs, metrics)
  - Instrument portal_server with OTel (traces, logs, metrics)
  - Upgrade dartastic_opentelemetry from 0.9.3 to 1.0.0-alpha and add OTel Logs signal
  - Implement all 5 CUR-734 custom application metrics
  - Add local OTel dev stack (Grafana LGTM) for development
  - Fix Cloud Run gRPC liveness probe for portal-service

  Phase 1: Planning & Infrastructure (CUR-1100)

  - prompts/o11y/plan.md — 7-phase observability implementation plan
  - tools/dev-env/docker-compose.otel.yml — Grafana LGTM stack (Tempo, Loki, Prometheus, Grafana) for local OTel development
  - portal-container/ — gRPC health check sidecar fix for Cloud Run liveness probes

  Phase 2: otel_common Shared Package (CUR-1100)

  New package at apps/common-dart/otel_common/ providing standardized OTel instrumentation for all Dart servers:

  ┌──────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │        Module        │                                           Purpose                                            │
  ├──────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ init.dart            │ initializeOTel() / shutdownOTel() — Cloud Run config, sampling, resource attributes          │
  ├──────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ middleware.dart      │ Shelf middleware: per-request spans, W3C TraceContext propagation, HTTP semantic conventions │
  ├──────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ db_tracing.dart      │ tracedQuery() wrapper with SQL sanitization (PII/PHI scrubbing)                              │
  ├──────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ logging.dart         │ Trace-correlated structured JSON logging for Cloud Logging                                   │
  ├──────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ error_reporting.dart │ Cloud Error Reporting format with PII scrubbing, span linkage                                │
  └──────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  - 47 unit + integration tests
  - README, demo server, local OTel demo script, coverage tooling

  Phase 3: Diary Server Instrumentation (CUR-1100)

  - diary_server/bin/server.dart — initializeOTel(), configureTracedLogging(), shutdownOTel()
  - diary_server/lib/src/server.dart — otelMiddleware() in request pipeline
  - diary_functions/lib/src/database.dart — all DB queries wrapped with tracedQuery()
  - All handlers instrumented with logWithTrace() and reportAndRecordError()
  - Local dev run scripts with OTel environment variables

  Upgrade to dartastic_opentelemetry 1.0.0-alpha (CUR-734)

  - otel_common/pubspec.yaml + append_only_datastore/pubspec.yaml — ^0.9.3 → ^1.0.0-alpha
  - Also upgrades dartastic_opentelemetry_api to 1.0.0-alpha, grpc to 5.1.0, protobuf to 6.0.0

  OTel Logs Signal (CUR-734)

  - init.dart — enableLogs: true, LoggerProvider shutdown
  - logging.dart — bridges every Dart Logger record to OTel Log Records via OTLP; maps Dart Level to OTel Severity; fixed Logger/LogRecord name collision between
  dartastic_opentelemetry and package:logging

  Portal Server Instrumentation (CUR-734)

  - portal_server/pubspec.yaml — added otel_common dependency
  - portal_server/bin/server.dart — replaced manual JSON logging with initializeOTel() + configureTracedLogging() + shutdownOTel()
  - portal_server/lib/src/server.dart — added otelMiddleware() to request pipeline

  CUR-734 Custom Metrics (5 metrics)

  ┌─────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │             Metric              │             Type             │                           Location                            │
  ├─────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ http_request_duration_seconds   │ histogram                    │ otel_common/middleware.dart (shared)                          │
  ├─────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ http_requests_total             │ counter (status/method/path) │ otel_common/middleware.dart (shared)                          │
  ├─────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ database_query_duration_seconds │ histogram                    │ otel_common/db_tracing.dart (shared)                          │
  ├─────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ active_sessions_count           │ gauge                        │ diary_functions/diary_metrics.dart + diary_server/server.dart │
  ├─────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ audit_events_written_total      │ counter                      │ diary_functions/diary_metrics.dart + user.dart                │
  └─────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  All instruments have // IMPLEMENTS: REQ-o00047 comments.

  Test Fixes

  - All test files using createServer() or tracedQuery() now initialize OTel in setUp:
    - diary_server/test/server_test.dart
    - diary_server/integration_test/test_server.dart
    - diary_functions/integration_test/{auth,database,user}_handlers_test.dart
    - portal_server/test/server_test.dart
    - portal_server/integration_test/test_server.dart

  Test Results

  - otel_common: 54/54 passed (47 existing + 7 new metrics tests)
  - diary_server: 33/33 passed
  - diary_functions: 113/113 passed
  - portal_server: 27/27 passed

  Known Gaps (follow-up — CUR-735)

  - Portal functions (notification_service.dart, questionnaire.dart) have no OTel spans or traced logging
  - Portal DB queries not wrapped with tracedQuery() (uses RLS context pattern)

Author: Michael Bushe
Message:
[CUR-825] EQ notification pipeline: FCM push notifications end-to-end. Implements REQ-CAL-p00023 (#324)

    Summary

    - Implements the full push notification pipeline for questionnaire
    alerts (EQ/NOSE/QoL)
    - Mobile app receives FCM tokens and registers them with the diary
    server
    - Portal server sends targeted FCM notifications when investigators
    assign questionnaires
    - Both servers share the patient_fcm_tokens table via the common
    PostgreSQL database
    - Adds questionnaire_instances table for tracking questionnaire
    lifecycle

 What's included

 Database (database/)
 - New questionnaire_type and questionnaire_status enums
    - New questionnaire_instances table with RLS policies (service_role +
    investigator SELECT)
    - New patient_fcm_tokens table with unique partial index for
    one-active-token-per-patient-per-platform
 - Standalone migration: 004_questionnaire_and_fcm_tokens.sql

 Diary Server (apps/daily-diary/diary_server/ + diary_functions/)
    - POST /api/v1/user/fcm-token — JWT-authenticated endpoint for mobile
    app to register FCM tokens
    - Upsert pattern: deactivates old token, inserts new one per
    patient+platform

 Portal Server (apps/sponsor-portal/portal_server/ + portal_functions/)
    - NotificationService — sends FCM via HTTP v1 API with Workload Identity
    Federation (no key files)
 - FCM_CONSOLE_MODE=true for local dev (logs instead of sending)
 - Questionnaire endpoints: send (POST), status (GET), delete (DELETE)
 - PostgreSQL enum type cast fixes for Dart postgres driver

 Mobile App (apps/daily-diary/clinical_diary/)
    - MobileNotificationService — handles FCM permissions, token lifecycle,
    foreground/background messages
    - TaskService — converts FCM data messages into actionable tasks with
    local persistence
 - TaskListWidget — displays pending questionnaire tasks on home screen
    - Auto-registers FCM token with diary server on startup and token
    refresh
 - Firebase config files for cure-hht-admin project (dev + QA flavors)

 Shared Types (apps/common-dart/trial_data_types/)
 - QuestionnaireInstance, QuestionnaireType, QuestionnaireStatus
 - Task, TaskType models

 Tooling
    - apps/sponsor-portal/tool/testNotification.sh — local test script for
    FCM flow

 Architecture

 Portal UI → Portal Server → questionnaire_instances (DB)
                           → patient_fcm_tokens (DB) → FCM HTTP v1 API
                                                             ↓
 Diary Server ← FCM token ← Mobile App ← Firebase Cloud Messaging

 Test plan

    - flutter analyze clean on mobile app, diary_functions, diary_server,
    portal_functions
 - All 13 home screen unit tests pass
 - macOS integration test pod conflict resolved (Firebase 12.4→12.8)
    - Run testNotification.sh against local portal server with
    FCM_CONSOLE_MODE=true
    - Verify FCM token registration on real device (USB install, linked
    patient)
 - Apply migration 004 to dev database
    - End-to-end: send questionnaire from portal → verify push received on
    device

 Implements: REQ-CAL-p00023, REQ-CAL-p00082, REQ-CAL-p00081, REQ-p00049

    ---------

    Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 Diary Server (apps/daily-diary/diary_server/ + diary_functions/)
    - POST /api/v1/user/fcm-token — JWT-authenticated endpoint for mobile
    app to register FCM tokens
    - Upsert pattern: deactivates old token, inserts new one per
    patient+platform

 Portal Server (apps/sponsor-portal/portal_server/ + portal_functions/)
    - NotificationService — sends FCM via HTTP v1 API with Workload Identity
    Federation (no key files)
 - FCM_CONSOLE_MODE=true for local dev (logs instead of sending)
 - Questionnaire endpoints: send (POST), status (GET), delete (DELETE)
 - PostgreSQL enum type cast fixes for Dart postgres driver

 Mobile App (apps/daily-diary/clinical_diary/)
    - MobileNotificationService — handles FCM permissions, token lifecycle,
    foreground/background messages
    - TaskService — converts FCM data messages into actionable tasks with
    local persistence
 - TaskListWidget — displays pending questionnaire tasks on home screen
    - Auto-registers FCM token with diary server on startup and token
    refresh
 - Firebase config files for cure-hht-admin project (dev + QA flavors)

 Shared Types (apps/common-dart/trial_data_types/)
 - QuestionnaireInstance, QuestionnaireType, QuestionnaireStatus
 - Task, TaskType models

 Tooling
    - apps/sponsor-portal/tool/testNotification.sh — local test script for
    FCM flow

 Architecture

 Portal UI → Portal Server → questionnaire_instances (DB)
                           → patient_fcm_tokens (DB) → FCM HTTP v1 API
                                                             ↓
 Diary Server ← FCM token ← Mobile App ← Firebase Cloud Messaging

    - flutter analyze clean on mobile app, diary_functions, diary_server,
    portal_functions
 - All 13 home screen unit tests pass
 - macOS integration test pod conflict resolved (Firebase 12.4→12.8)
    - Run testNotification.sh against local portal server with
    FCM_CONSOLE_MODE=true
    - Verify FCM token registration on real device (USB install, linked
    patient)
 - Apply migration 004 to dev database
    - End-to-end: send questionnaire from portal → verify push received on
    device

Author: Michael Bushe
Message: Feat/cur 629 first admin (#236) First Admin Dashboard for Sponsor Portal Implemented first admin login functionality for the clinical trial sponsor portal with GCP Cloud SQL RLS (Row-Level Security) enforcement and admin dashboard UI. Requirements Implemented - REQ-d00031: Identity Platform Integration - REQ-d00032: Role-Based Access Control Implementation - REQ-d00035: Admin Dashboard Implementation - REQ-d00036: User Management Interface - REQ-p00005: Role-Based Access Control - REQ-p00014: Least Privilege Access - REQ-p00024: Portal User Roles and Permissions Initial Portal Projects: apps/sponsor-portal/portal-ui - Sponsor Portal Flutter Web App apps/sponsor-portal/portal_functions - Cloud Run Functions apps/sponsor-portal/portal_server - Cloud Run Server apps/sponsor-portal/portal-container - Cloud Run Container All with unit, integration tests passing over 85%, portal functions has a new pipeline: .github/workflows/sponsor-portal-ci.yml Local and cicd builds use the Firebase Emulator (emulates GCP Identity Platform): tools/dev-env/docker-compose.firebase.yml All projects, and the portal directory, have README explaining usage and purpose. Seed Data in hht_diary_callisto and (new) hht_diary_curehht repos, loads the first admin email: database/README.md database/seed_data.sql database/seed_data_prod.sql database/seed_data_qa.sql database/seed_data_uat.sql Database has new: portal_users table, portal_user_site_access (portal users to asigned sites) portal_user_role table: CREATE TYPE portal_user_role AS ENUM ( 'Investigator', 'Sponsor', 'Auditor', 'Analyst', 'Administrator', 'Developer Admin' ); sponsor_role_mapping, the curehht mapping looks like the above, the callisto mapping is CRA, Admin, etc., populated from seed data in the private sponsor repos) Portal UI: Login page, Admin home page The portal ui is deployed inside the portal container and served by nginx. Since it's low traffic and not frequently updated, by design, it's a simple approach that's good enough. CORS works automatically, as opposed to extra config if it was hosted from a storage bucket. User login passes the user token, selected role and site to the server on all calls which is propagate to the postgresql calling context for RLS RBAC and auditing. Files: - portal_functions/lib/src/database.dart - Added UserContext class and executeWithContext() method What it does: - Uses GCP Cloud SQL requires explicit session context setting - Added two-level role system: - PostgreSQL roles (authenticated, service_role) for RLS policy selection - Application roles (Administrator, Investigator, etc.) for policy conditions - Session variables set via SET LOCAL ROLE and set_config() within transactions 2. Database RLS Policy Fixes Files: - database/rls_policies.sql - Fixed service_role policies Changes: - Changed portal_users_service_update to portal_users_service_all (ALL operations instead of just UPDATE) - Added portal_site_access_service_all policy for site access during login 3. Portal Auth Updates Files: - portal_functions/lib/src/portal_auth.dart - Updated to use UserContext.service for login/linking operations 4. RLS Integration Tests Files: - portal_functions/integration_test/rls_context_test.dart - NEW (22 tests) Test coverage: - Session context variables (user_id, role, allowed_roles) - Service context access (read/update all users) - Administrator role access (see all users, create users) - Investigator role restrictions (only see own record, cannot insert) - Auditor role access (read-only all users) - Context isolation between queries - RLS helper functions existence - RLS policies existence verification 5. Documentation Files: - apps/sponsor-portal/README.md - Added "Security Architecture: RLS with GCP Cloud SQL" section Documents: - Two-level role system explanation - Session context variables table - Code examples for service vs authenticated context - RLS policy examples 6. Admin Dashboard UI Files: - portal-ui/lib/widgets/portal_app_bar.dart - NEW - App bar with user info and logout - portal-ui/lib/pages/admin/admin_dashboard_page.dart - REWRITTEN - Full admin dashboard - portal-ui/lib/router/app_router.dart - Added /admin route and placeholder routes Features: - Role-based authentication check - Role banner showing current role - Navigation rail (Overview, Users, Sites) - Welcome message with quick action cards - Sign out functionality with redirect to login Test Results - portal_functions: 161 tests passing (including 22 new RLS tests) - portal-ui: 56 tests passing - flutter analyze: No issues End-to-End Flow 1. User opens portal → Login page 2. Enter credentials → Firebase Auth emulator authenticates 3. Server receives token → Looks up/links user in portal_users 4. RLS context set for all subsequent queries 5. Administrator redirected to /admin dashboard 6. Dashboard displays with navigation rail and welcome message --------- Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5

Author: Developer
Message:
[CUR-423] Add hht_auth libraries for Web Diary authentication

Implement three-package Dart authentication system:

- hht_auth_core: Shared models and interfaces (AuthToken, WebUser,
  SponsorConfig, SponsorPattern, AuthResult, etc.)
- hht_auth_server: Cloud Run auth service (JWT signing, Argon2 verification,
  rate limiting, user/pattern repositories)
- hht_auth_client: Flutter Web client (session management, inactivity tracking,
  storage clearing, Riverpod/Signals adapters)

Key features:
- Sponsor identification via linking code pattern matching
- Argon2id password hashing (client-side before transmission)
- JWT tokens with 15-minute expiry (RS256 signing)
- Auto-logout on inactivity (configurable 1-30 min, default 2)
- Complete browser storage clearing on logout
- Sponsor config fetched directly from Sponsor Portal

Tests: 33 (core) + 48 (server) passing; client tests require Flutter SDK

Implements: REQ-d00077, REQ-d00078, REQ-d00079, REQ-d00080, REQ-d00081,
            REQ-d00082, REQ-d00083, REQ-d00084

plugins and prevents secrets from appearing in process lists, logs, or git history.
